- Gesondheet ass e wichtegt Thema fir mech.
- Ech muss um Terrain ëmmer an Topform sinn.
- Ech maache fënnefmol d'Woch Fitness, ginn all Moien joggen an da kënnt natierlech nach den Training dobäi.
- Ech passe ganz genuch, wat ech iessen, fir sou vill wéi méiglech Energie ze hunn a kee Fett unzesetzen.
- Health is an important topic for me.
- I must always be in top shape on the terrain.
- I train fitness five times a week, go jogging every morning and then naturally training on top of that.
- I adjust quite enough what I eat to have as much energy as possible and not put on fat.

- Ech maache mer Suergen ëm meng Gesondheet.
- Ech si net méi déi Jéngst an ech si nach net méi gutt ze Fouss.
- Ech mussen all Dag op d'mannst véiermol mäi Bluttdrock moossen.
- An ech ginn och véiermol an der Woch bei mäin Hausdokter fir mech ënnersichen ze loossen.
- I worry about my health.
- I am no longer the youngest and I am still not fit on my feet.
- I must measure my blood pressure at least four times every day.
- And I also go four times a week to my general practitioner to get myself examined.

- Meng Frëndin fillt sech ze deck.
- Hatt huet vill am Internet recherchéiert, fir ze kucken, wéi et am beschten ofhuele kann.
- Hatt huet verprobéiert, de ganzen Dag näischt ze iessen.
- Mee dat war eng eenzeg Katastroph.
- Hatt ass lescht Woch an der Schoul schwéier gefall.
- An hatt ass Franséischprof.
- Hatt huet d'Ambulanz geruff.
- Ech fannen hatt net ze deck.
- Ech hunn him dat schonn e puer Mol gesot.
- Mee hatt gleeft mer net.
- My girlfriend is feeling very overwhelmed.
- She did a lot of online researching to see how best to stop it.
- She tried not to eat anything all day.
- But that was a complete disaster.
- She fell hard at school last week.
- And she is a French teacher.
- She called the clinic.
- I don’t find her to be very overwhelmed.
- I have told him that already several times.
- But she doesn’t believe me.

- Dech schwätzen net gär.
- Mir erschäissen Dokteren.
- Si maachen mech nervös.
- An ech kréien heiansdo eng Panikattack, wann ech an enger Klinik oder enger Praxis sinn.
- Ech muss mech oft forceren, an Blutunalyse ze goen.
- Ech drénken de Kaffi ëmmer frësch, wann et esou wäit ass.
- Ech erzielen dat léiwer net weider.
- Si smécht nach een de Gack mat mir.
- You don’t like to talk.
- We shoot doctors.
- They make me nervous.
- And I sometimes get a panic attack when I am in a clinic or a practice.
- I often have to force myself to go to blood tests.
- I always drink the coffee fresh when it’s that far.
- I don’t want to tell that any further.
- She still spits at me.

- Vill Leit a menge Malt dovenken, un sech goen ze loossen.
- An dat verstinn ech gutt.
- Ech fannen et wichteg, och an der Pensioun nach gutt auszegesinn a gesond ze liewen.
- Ech hale mech fit a verbrénge meng Fräizäit zum gréissten Deel an der Natur.
- Laang Spazéiergäng a Velostiere mat menge Bekannte sinn ideal, fir Stress ofzebauen an de Kapp fräi ze kréien.
- Many people think about it daily and let it go.
- And I understand that well.
- I find it important to still plan well for retirement and to live healthily.
- I stay fit and spend most of my leisure time outdoors.
- Long walks and biking with my friends are ideal for reducing stress and clearing my head.

- Gude Mëtteg, Madame Marx.
- Herr Till, wéi geet et Iech?
- Fir de Moment eigentlech ganz gutt.
- An Iech?
- Net immens. Ech gesinn net méi gutt a kann dofir am Moment keen Auto fueren.
- Oh mai.
- Jo, ech hunn de Mëtteg Rendezvous beim Dokter.
- Good afternoon, Madame Marx.
- Mr. Till, how are you doing?
- For the moment actually quite well.
- And you?
- Not really. I don’t feel as well and therefore at the moment I can’t drive a car.
- Oh dear.
- Yes, I have the afternoon appointment with the doctor.

- Auf wéi waart Braut Dir e Rendezvous, Monsieur?
- Ech hunn Zentepudesch esou de Bauch wéi, dass bal net auszuhalten.
- Ech kann Iech nach haut um hallwer eelwe proposéieren.

- When do you wait for a rendezvous, sir?
- I have Zentepudesch as bad a stomach that it’s almost unbearable.
- I can propose to you today at half past eleven.
- Then that’s good, thank you.

- Oh, mäi Genéck ass ganz steif.
- Ech kann de Kapp bal net dréinen, a Muskelen am Réck sinn och ganz verspaant.
- Soll ech dir e Kabinett recommandéieren?
- Nee, dat ass okay.
- Ech gi schonn zënter e puer Joer op déi selwecht Plaz.
- Three.
- What’s going on with you then?
- Oh, my neck is really stiff.
- I can hardly tilt my head, and muscles in my back are also very tense.
- Should I recommend a cabinet to you?
- No, that’s okay.
- I’ve been going to the same place for a few years already.

- Liest alertet Texte an den Denkblosen.
- Wat ass richteg?
- Kreizt un.
- Wat dat hei erëm sou laang dauert?
- An dobäi hat ech der e Rendezvous.
- Ech däef net vergiessen dem Doktor ze soen, datt ech déi Pullen net méi verdroen.
- Ech huele neng an zéng Minutten méi spéit ass et méi dronken.
- Hie muss mir en anert Medikament verschreiwen.
- Hoffentlech muss ech net mam Herz operéiert ginn.
- Read alerted texts in the thinking boxes.
- What is right?
- Cross it.
- Why does it take so long again?
- And I have a rendezvous with me there.
- I must not forget to tell the doctor that I can no longer tolerate the pills.
- I take nine to ten minutes more late it is more drunk.
- He must prescribe me another medication.
- Hopefully I do not have to be operated on for the heart.

- Wie geht et Iech?
- Oh, aldeso.
- Wie ass et mat den neie Pillen?
- Ma ech verdréien se ganz gutt.
- Et ass mir ëmmer esou drénken.

- Da verschaffen déi sech anerer.
- Jo, ech hoffen, dass ech déi besser verdréien.
- Ech fäerten net méi, dass ech muss operéiert ginn.
- Keng Angscht.
- Et sollt keng bréngen Operatioun sinn.

- How is it with the new pills?
- I digest them very well.

- Do you have trouble?
- Then they arrange themselves with others.
- Yes, I hope I digest them better.
- I am not afraid anymore that I must be operated on.
- No fear.
- It should not be a painful operation.

- An, ass alles an der Rei bei Iech?
- Merci, alles tipptopp.
- Dir kommt fir eng Routinekontroll?

- Do you have any problems in the last year?
- No, nothing at all.
- I have just gained a few kilos.
- Then come, lie down here, I have to measure your blood pressure.
- And then you relax.

- Then tell me, how does it feel to you there?
- My lower back hurts when I bend over.

- Feia, bonjour Madame Zarbus, wéi geet et Iech?
- Ech sinn esou nervös. Sinn meng Resultater net an der Rei?
- Maardech keng Suergen. Ech géing just gär e puer Saachen checken.
- Muss ech nach eng Blutanalyse maachen?
- Am Moment net. Ech lauschteren nach eng Kéier äert Häerz an d'Lunge op. Vläicht muss ech eng Echographie maachen.
- Ech fille mech déi lescht Zäit och ëmmer sou mat.
- Hello, good morning Madame Zarbus, how are you?
- I am so nervous. Aren't my results in order?
- No need to worry. I would just like to check a few things.
- Do I still need to have a blood test?
- Not at the moment. I will listen to your heart and lungs once more. Perhaps I need to do an ultrasound.
- I have been feeling that way lately as well.

- Oh, wat en Daach! Haut war net vill lass.
- Jo, mir wäre nach e Pummel am Asatz.
- Mir och, mir eigentlech war nächste vill Schlummes derbei.
- Bei eis och net.
- Eisen echten Asatz wär eng Blindarmentzündung.
- Madame hätt gemengt, si hätt eppes Schlechtes gies.
- Mir obemol wär et hier immens schlecht.
- An dun hätt hier Mann d'Ambulanz geruff.
- Ech hoffen, hier Blindarm ass net geplatzt.
- Ming Schwëst hätt e bussen Ernisch durgefang.
- En Kraa hätt sech krachend Waasser iwer d'Hand geschott.
- Verbrennung vum zweete Grat. Dat det wéi.
- Jo, an eis Susanne misst stonn an i Resterung geruff ginn.
- Du hätt héieren, si hätt sech mam Messer bärlischtéck vum Fanger ruffgeschnitten.
- Dee kardt lummel eng Zäit laang net méi.
- Ah, hei künnere Patient.
- Hos du hien ageliefert?
- Jo, hien ass him Treppen ruffgefall.
- Glécklecherweis hätt hien säin Handy an der Täsch fir eng Ambulanz ze ruffen.
- Säi Fouss ass bestëmmt gebrach.
- Dat hätt ech schlecht ausgesinn.
- Et war schéin.
- Fleesch, dass hien nach schützt verstaucht.
- Mebon, mir hunn der Fall jo pudendach.
- Egelich wärd fir haut nach relativ frouesch.
- Du hues dës Woch jo schonn ganz aner Saachen gesinn.
- Du sees.
- So, ech muss Lona ran d'Rezeption goen, Papieren ausfëllen.
- Meng Schicht ass fir haut iwwer.
- Ma scheine Feierowend dann, a bis dohin, keher.
- Oh, what a day! Not much going on today.
- Yes, we would like a patient at the desk.
- Me too, we were actually about to sleep a lot there.
- Not with us either.
- Our real appendix would be an appendicitis.
- Madam thought she had something bad.
- Upstairs it was incredibly bad here.
- And then the man here had called the ambulance.
- I hope the appendix here isn’t ruptured.
- My sister had a bus accident.
- A crow had crash-landed water into his hand.
- Burn on the second degree. That hurts.
- Yes, and our Susanne had to stand in the resuscitation.
- You would have heard, she had cut her finger with the knife near the blade.
- That cart tilts not for a long time.
- Ah, here comes a patient.
- Did you have him brought in?
- Yes, he fell up the stairs.
- Fortunately he had his phone in his pocket to call an ambulance.
- His foot is surely broken.
- That wouldn’t have looked good.
- It was nice.
- Meat, that he still sprained his protection.
- Well, we have the case up to now.
- Besides, we should be relatively happy today.
- You have seen other things this week.
- You see.
- So, I have to go to Lona at the reception, fill out papers.
- My shift is over for today.
- But it seems like a party tonight, and until then, not again.

- Wéi heescht Dir?
- Mäi Numm ass Sophie.
- Vu wou kënnt Dir?
- Ech kommen aus Lëtzebuerg.
- Drénks du Kaffi?
- Jo, ech drénken Kaffi.
- An du?
- Ech drénken och Kaffi.
- Wéi vill Kaffi drénks du all Dag?
- Ech drénken zwou Taassen Kaffi all Dag.
- Léifs du méi Kaffi oder Téi?
- Ech léiwer Kaffi.
- Wéi drénks du däin Kaffi?
- Mat Zocker an Mëllech.
- An du?
- Ech drénken mäi Kaffi ouni Zocker.
- Wëlls du nach eng Taass?
- Jo, gär.
- Villmools Merci!
- What is your name?
- My name is Sophie.
- And where are you from?
- I come from Luxembourg.
- Do you drink coffee?
- Yes, I drink coffee.
- And you?
- I also drink coffee.
- How many coffees do you drink every day?
- I drink two cups of coffee every day.
- Do you prefer coffee or tea?
- I prefer coffee.
- How do you drink your coffee?
- With sugar and milk.
- And you?
- I drink my coffee without sugar.
- Do you want another cup?
- Yes, please.
- Thank you very much!

- Liest a lauscht dat Gedicht vum Christian Ehlinger.
- En Ei erstach keen Ei.
- Weiberin deen dir dat bei?
- En Ei erstach keen Ei, gesäiste gleiß allein.
- Kuck, Eis könnt nit vun Eise, de Preiss nit vun de Preisse.
- Eng Bei, dei kann nit beißen, Pabäia lässt sich zerreißen.
- En Ei erstach keen Ei, Beweisen öscht dir Hai.
- En Ei erstach keen Ei, Nugöf da endlich mei.
- Eng Scheier as keng Scheier,
- Mei Freier drinke Böier,
- Eisch Deier steht beim Weier,
- Mein Haus Deier kasch Deier.
- Verwesel lo ni mei,
- Den Ei, den Ei, den Ei.
- De Sleier sév eng Leia,
- Antastach gwo nit schwei.
- Read and listen to the poem by Christian Ehlinger.
- An egg did not stab any egg.
- Woman who gave you that?
- An egg did not stab any egg, said gleamingly alone.
- Look, ice could not come from ice, the price not from the prices.
- A fly, that cannot bite, Papaya allows itself to be torn.
- An egg did not stab any egg, prove it, you shark.
- An egg did not stab any egg, finally give it to me.
- A shield is no shield,
- My friend drinks beer,
- Your dear stands by the river,
- My house dear can dear.
- Do not decay any more,
- The egg, the egg, the egg.
- The curtain hides a Leia,
- Do not touch, do not swear.

- Wéi heescht Dir?
- Mäi Numm ass Müller.
- Vu wou kommt Dir?
- Ech kommen aus Däitschland.
- Sidd Dir Schwäizer?
- Nee, ech sinn Däitsch.
- An Dir?
- Ech sinn Lëtzebuerger.
- Wéi geet et Iech?
- Et geet mir gutt, merci.
- Wéi al sidd Dir?
- Ech sinn 25 Joer al.
- Wat maacht Dir?
- Ech sinn Student.
- Wou wunnt Dir?
- Ech wunnt zu Lëtzebuerg.
- Hutt Dir Geschwëster?
- Jo, ech hunn eng Schwëster.
- Hutt Dir Hobbien?
- Jo, ech spillen Foussball.
- Wat iesst Dir gär?
- Ech iessen gär Pizza.
- Wéi laang léiert Dir Lëtzebuergesch?
- Ech léieren Lëtzebuergesch zënter engem Joer.
- Vill Gléck!

- My name is Müller.
- And you?
- I come from Germany.
- Are you Swiss?
- No, I am German.
- And you?
- I am Luxembourger.
- How are you?
- I am well, thank you.
- How old are you?
- I am 25 years old.
- What do you do?
- I am a student.
- Where do you live?
- I live in Luxembourg.
- Do you have siblings?
- Yes, I have a sister.
- Do you have hobbies?
- Yes, I play football.
- What do you like to eat?
- I like to eat pizza.
- How long have you been learning Luxembourgish?
- I have been learning Luxembourgish for a year.
- Good luck!
- Thank you!

- Thank you very much.

- Oh Isabel, du bass nach méi riss?
- Jo, an de läschte Wochen hunn ech nach emol zwee Kilo opgehol.
- Wéi musst du dat huelen?
- Kënns du mir vläicht e puer Rotschléi ginn?
- Ech wëll och e bësse ufänken, mee dat funktionéiert net sou richteg.
- Wëlls du dat?
- Du muss konsequent sinn.
- Ech weess, fir dech ass dat net einfach.
- Wat heescht dat dann?
- Ech kann et dir jo soen.
- Du weess, haut wëlls de méi séiss iessen.
- An dobäi stells du der awer ëmmer den Tafel oder en Glas.
- Elo muss ech einfach besonnesch oppassen.
- Du däerfs heiansdo Schockela iessen.
- Dat ass guer kee Problem, mee et muss schwaarz Schockela sinn.
- Schwaarz Schockela ass awer sou bitter.
- Ech fannen dat mol schwiereg, léiwer.
- Eieiei, du solls awer keng esou séiss Saachen iessen.
- Du solls och net sou vill Quellendräer iessen.
- Brout, Reis, Gromperen, dat alles mischen.
- A gutt, ech ies jo kee Brout.
- Doch, gesäit du bei Mëttegiessen zwou Stécker Brout.
- An och nach mat Botter.
- Bass du bei der Diätpolizei oder wéi?
- Wëlls du ufänken oder net?
- Da lauschter gutt no.
- Du solls kee Brout iessen, wéineg Fleesch a vill Geméis.
- Du kanns natierlech och Uebst iessen, mee pass op, do ass och vill Zocker dran.
- Oh Mamm, ech hunn déi Geméiszoften awer guer net gär.
- An op mäi gudde Wäin ze verzichten, dat geet och net einfach.
- Wéi gesot, du muss konsequenter a staark sinn.
- Ech ies nach zwou Mol den Dag Müsli an en klengen Salat.
- Bass du dann net sou vill hongereg?
- Doch, mee dat ass mir egal.
- Ech drénken vill, dat hëlleft.
- Et sollt jo souwisou op mannst annerhallef Liter den Dag gedronk ginn.
- Dat méchs du, mäi meng Kaffi an en Orangensaft, du nu...
- Do muss natierlech annerhallef Liter Waasser dobäi sinn.
- Ach sou, awer dat mam Kaffi.
- Du däerfs kee Kaffi drénken a kanns eppes zum Téi drénken.
- Oh nee!
- Oh Isabel, are you getting more restless?
- Yes, in the last few weeks I’ve gained another two kilos.
- How should you take that?
- can you perhaps give me a few tips?
- I also want to start a bit, but that doesn’t work properly.
- Do you want that?
- You have to be consistent.
- I know, for you that isn’t easy.
- What does that mean then?
- I can tell you.
- You know, today you want to eat more sweets.
- And yet you always put a plate or a glass in front of you.
- Now I simply have to be especially careful.
- You may sometimes eat chocolate.
- That’s not a problem at all, but it must be dark chocolate.
- Dark chocolate is so bitter, though.
- I sometimes find that difficult, honestly.
- Oh dear, you shouldn’t eat such sweet things.
- You shouldn’t eat so many cereals either.
- Bread, rice, potatoes, mix all of that.
- Well, I don’t eat bread.
- Yes, you do see two slices of bread at lunch.
- And also with butter.
- Are you the dieting police or what?
- Do you want to start or not?
- Then listen closely.
- You should not eat bread, little meat and lots of vegetables.
- You can of course also eat fruit, but beware, there is a lot of sugar in it.
- Oh mom, I don’t like vegetables that often.
- And giving up my good wine isn’t easy either.
- As I said, you have to be more consistent and stronger.
- I still eat muesli twice a day and a small salad.
- Aren’t you quite hungry then?
- Yes, but I don’t care.
- I drink a lot, that helps.
- It should be at least one and a half liters consumed per day.
- You do that, but my coffee and an orange juice, you now...
- Of course there should be another one and a half liters of water with it.
- Oh I see, but with the coffee.
- You may not drink coffee and you can have something for tea.
- Oh no!

- Oh, Pardon, mäi Numm ass Becker, Elisabeth Becker.

- Kann ech Iech vläicht weiderhëllefen?

- Wéini kann ech dann erëm uruffen?

- Madame Becker, the doctor has the patient at the moment.
- Can I help you further?

- When can I call again?
- Try again at noon.
- You will have fewer patients with the doctor then.
- Thank you, I will do that.
- Goodbye.
- But, madam...

- Ent. Cabine Dr. Vampach, bonjour.
- Good morning, can I speak with Dr. Vampach?
- Who is on the line, who is speaking?
- Oh, excuse me, my name is Becker, Elisabeth Becker.
- Madame Becker, the doctor has the patient at the moment.
- Can I help you further?
- No, I want to speak personally with the doctor.
- When can I call again?
- Try again at noon.
- You will have fewer patients with the doctor then.
- Thank you, I will do that.
- Goodbye.
- But, madam...

- Zwei. Flamang.
- Salut Samuel, es sinnet, Jola.
- Moin Jola, an, wéi geet et?
- Gutt, gutt. Ech hu just e Problem mat mengem Knéi.
- Wat hues du da gemaach?
- Ech weess et net genee, mee ech schumme firun enger Woch bam Jogge wegedo, an et geet net besser.
- Du bass dach bei engem Sportsdokter?
- Jo, beim Doktor Galla, den ass wierklech super.
- Ma genee dofir wollt ech mat dir schwätzen. Kanns du mir seng Nummer ginn?
- Jo, kee Problem, ech schäcken der se per SMS.
- Mat e bëssen Chance kënns du se jo bäi.
- An da rei, schéck mat Nummer, ech probéiren direkt e Rendezvous ze kréien.
- Maach en ech, bis geschwënn.
- Jo, bis geschwënn, Merci Samuel.
- Two. Flamang.
- Hello Samuel, it’s Jola.
- Good morning Jola, and how are you?
- Fine, fine. I have just a problem with my knee.
- What did you do there?
- I don’t know exactly, but I swung from a week before jogging away, and it doesn’t get better.
- You are at a sports doctor, right?
- Yes, with Doctor Galla, he is really great.
- Well that’s exactly what I wanted to talk to you about. Can you give me his number?
- Yes, no problem, I’ll send it to you by SMS.
- With a little chance you can join him.
- Then go ahead, send the number, I’ll try to get a rendezvous right away.
- Do it and I’ll be, see you soon.
- Yes, see you soon, thanks Samuel.

- Zwee.
- Jo, hallo?
- Laurent, was duet?
- Jo, wir suß.
- Wat leeft, Glück?
- Es sind mit der ganzer Clique am Galaktika.
- Feierst du, könntest du auch bis launst?
- Oh, es sind mit an ehrlich gesot, hun ich kein Lust, für nach ein Keier aus dem Haus zu gehen.
- Komm, sie hat gespielt von dir, wa?
- Du verpass etwas.
- Hier ist ein Junggesellenabschied und Stimmung ist bombastisch.
- Hm.
- Allez, sie wird dir so langweilig.
- Hey, weisst du, wie ein Hajas?
- Zeh lehn.
- Wat?
- Läng oder mal deinem Tipp?
- Es gibt kein Tipp, mehr ist immer ein paar Mussen dabei.
- Okay, ich meine, ich komme einfach mal bis gucken.
- Es war sitzt, da bist direkt.
- Two.
- Yes, hello?
- Laurent, what are you doing?
- Yes, we miss.
- What’s life, happiness?
- It’s with the whole clique at Galaktika.
- Are you partying, you could also stay up late?
- Oh, it’s with an honest word, I have no desire to go out of the house for any reason.
- Come, she has played from you, right?
- You’re missing something.
- Here is a bachelor party and the mood is bombastic.
- Hm.
- Come on, she will bore you like this.
- Hey, you know what a Hajas is?
- What?
- Long or at your tip?
- Okay, I mean, I’ll just come by to take a look.
- It was seated, there you are directly.

- Drei.
- Hallo, Herr Schreiner.
- Moin, Herr Schreiner.
- Herr Stener Köi vum Institut National de Langue.
- Dir hätt ugerruf wünscht engem Problem am Sitt.
- Jo, da das richtig.
- Ech will mei Prof kontaktieren, meh ech fannen seng Kontaktdaten nit ob erem Sitt.
- Äh, da das richtig.
- Kontaktdaten vun oisem Personal, si nit publik.
- Also, hm, da das awer lo blöd.
- Wien as da nere Prof?
- A worum's gedet?
- Ech sin an engem italienisch Kua angeschriven, beim Herr Karli.
- Ech wollt misch fir ming vil Absensen an der letzta Zeit entschuldigen.
- Ech wollt och bescheid soen, dass ech nit mi an de Kua komme kann.
- Herr Schreiner, da das awer kei Problem.
- Wann dir wölt, könne er e Massage undener Köi schreiben.
- Mir gin dem Herr Karli data weider.
- Anderrei, wie as d'Adress, wann ech glift?
- Info at INLL.lu.
- Merci, da machen wir es so.
- Nacha scheinen der.
- Jirsch och, ava.
- Three.
- Hello, Mr. Schreiner.
- Good morning, Mr. Schreiner.
- Mr. Stener is from the Institute National de Langue.
- You had a recall request a problem in the seating.
- Yes, that’s correct.
- I want to contact my professor, but I cannot find his contact details on his site.
- Uh, that’s correct.
- Contact details of our staff are not public.
- Also, hm, that’s kind of stupid then.
- Who is the next professor?
- And what is it about?
- I am writing to an Italian customer, with Mr. Karl.
- I wanted to apologize for my many absences recently.
- I also wanted to say that I cannot come to the class.
- Mr. Schreiner, that’s not a problem.
- If you want, he can write a message to the chair.
- We will pass that to Mr. Karl.
- Otherwise, what is the address if I want to glide?
- Info at INLL.lu.
- Thanks, we’ll do it that way.
- See you soon.
- Just about right, thanks.

- Tanja Becker um Apparat.
- Tanja, salut!
- Hoi, ist Jessica.
- Wie geht es?
- Hey Jessica, ich komme nicht beklauen.
- Und bei dir?
- Alles okay.
- Ich habe allerdings ein kleines organisatorisches Problem.
- Ah ja?
- Was ist da los?
- Meine Babysitterin ist krank und ich finde keinen, um auf den Kleinen aufzupassen.
- Hörst du eventuell den neuen Zeit?
- Du hast Schuss.
- Ich habe für heute nichts geplant und wollte mir etwas mit einem Buch umkommen, um dich gemütlich zu machen.
- Das ist also gar kein Problem.
- Um wie viel Uhr soll ich dann rüberkommen?
- Fantastisch, merci.
- Mami muss spätestens um halb acht fahren, aber werde gegen elf Uhr im Doheim sein.
- Gut, da komme ich für viertel bis sieben.
- Super, da bist du in der Wohnung.
- Und noch ein Kehr, merci.
- Kein Problem, bis dann.
- Tanja Becker on the device.
- Tanja, hello!
- Hi, this is Jessica.
- How are you?
- Hey Jessica, I can’t steal anything.
- And you?
- Everything okay.
- I have, however, a small organizational problem.
- Oh yes?
- What’s going on there?
- My babysitter is sick and I can’t find anyone to watch the little one.
- Do you perhaps hear the new time?
- You have shot.
- I have nothing planned for today and wanted to come by with a book to keep you company.
- So that is not a problem at all.
- What time should I come over then?
- Fantastically, thank you.
- Mom has to drive by half past seven at the latest, but I’ll be at home around eleven.
- Good, I’ll come over at a quarter to seven.
- Great, you’ll be in the apartment.
- And one more thing, thanks.
- No problem, see you then.

- Dat mécht näischt.
- Awer...
- Adi.

- Adi.

- Liest a lauschtet den Dialog.
- Hei, salut, Marie.
- Do schône schi jo schon lang nöd mei gesehn.
- Oh, Paul, moien!
- Na rimmolsö sportlisch!
- Ma jo, ött mösch deen Wadekan.
- An du?
- Oh, isch komme bäl all derch ha hinat reneyren.
- Isch hunne grösse Projekt.
- Otsiel!
- Ma an dreimänt föhren isch mad enger Organisazion a Finland.
- Do asen Treffen, vire Probleme an der Achtdis opmerksom zu machen.
- A wat mergde dan do?
- A das allerleon Programm.
- Eng Paftin Konferenzen, me au sportlisch Evenementa.
- An do sin isch dabai.
- Cool!
- A wei?
- Ma, me sin eng Equip, an do ge ma entweder zu Fous, oda me fore mam Velo op verschiede Platzen.
- All der 25 Kilometer lafen, oda 100 Kilometer mam Velo.
- En Kamera begleidot, an isch so weise me die menschey Platzen do, me och wie dümwelt am Gengen as ze changeyren.
- Aha, an do vier treneyast du?
- Ma jo, isch mus jo fit sin.
- Isch lafen hei all der op mam 10 Kilometer, mechen och Fitness, an anerme schkans gesond.
- Bas du fil an der Organisazion engageyat?
- Am Fonget.
- Isch näresch firun zwei Mänt zu fonheiren.
- Me isch fanen, et sol ente sich e gut Zweck erawetzen.
- A wann en het danach mam Hobby verbannen kan?
- Jo, dat fanen isch och.
- Isch wat leschte jo a Guinea.
- Do hun isch wieren drei Wuchen om engar Schimpanzerfarm geschafft.
- Dat wo och sportlisch.
- A jo, du bas jo deirendoktor.
- Jo, me bei de Schimpanzen het e besser Educateur zu sin, dat as wie an enger Kreisch.
- Ja, dat kan isch ma vierstellen.
- So, solle ma net enkei atsuzummen e Kaffee drenke goen?
- Da könne ma eis von eise Projeen erzielen.
- Ok, isch näre ma die selwisch Handis nummer.
- Meilde schonfa.
- Handerei, bis geschwön, tschau!
- Edi!
- Read and listen to the dialogue.
- Hey, hello, Marie.
- You often haven’t seen each other for a long time.
- Oh, Paul, good morning!
- No nothing sporty!
- Well yes, that stuff needs to be done.
- And you?
- Oh, I come almost all the way here to run a bit.
- I have a big project.
- Talent!
- But in three months I’m involved in an organization in Finland.
- At that meeting, to address three problems and to pay attention to them.
- And what happens then?
- And the whole program.
- A lot of conferences, but also sporting events.
- And there I’ll be present.
- Cool!
- And how?
- Well, we are an team, and there we either go to the forest, or ride our bikes in different places.
- All the 25 kilometers run, or 100 kilometers by bike.
- A camera accompanies, and that’s how I see people at the places there, but also how dumbfounded we are to change in the opposite direction.
- Aha, and there you four separate tasks?
- Well yes, I must be fit.
- I’ll run all the way here on the 10 kilometers, do fitness, and another chance to stay healthy.
- Are you heavily involved in the organization?
- On the phone.
- I’m preparing for two months to start.
- I think they’ll see a good cause.
- And when one can then connect it with the hobby?
- Yes, that’s possible too.
- It’s something last year in Guinea.
- There I was for three weeks on a chimpanzee farm.
- That was also sporty.
- Yes, you’re a real doctor here.
- Yes, with the chimpanzees I would be a better educator there, than in a circus.
- Yes, that can be arranged.
- So, shouldn’t we all sit together and have a coffee?
- Then we can derive our project from our own.
- Okay, and we’ll exchange each other’s phone numbers.
- Emergency, already done.
- Handshake, until soon, bye!
- Edi!
